page.content: The analysis presents two tables, the first detailing beliefs and levels of agreement, with headers including Belief*, Strongly Disagree (Rating 1–2) N (%), Neutral Range (Rating 3–5) N (%), and Strongly Agree (Rating 6–7) N (%). The data shows for Belief 1, 270 (68.2) strongly disagree, 121 (30.5) are neutral, and 5 (1.3) strongly agree; for Belief 2, 19 (4.8) strongly disagree, 245 (61.7) are neutral, and 133 (33.5) strongly agree; for Belief 3, 252 (63.5) strongly disagree, 73 (34.5) are neutral, and 8 (2.0) strongly agree; for Belief 4, 61 (15.4) strongly disagree, 232 (58.8) are neutral, and 102 (25.8) strongly agree. The second table provides data on C57BL/6 and LDLr−/− with measurements under OVA− and OVA+. In the liver, total lipids are 126 (15) mg/g for C57BL/6 OVA−, 141 (9) mg/g for C57BL/6 OVA+, 123 (7) mg/g for LDLr−/− OVA−, and 123 (9) mg/g for LDLr−/− OVA+; cholesterol values are 6.4 (1.1), 7.4 (1.0), 7.1 (1.0), and 7.0 (1.1) mg/g respectively. In the cecum, total lipids are 39.0 (5.4), 38.7 (9.5), 42.8 (10.1), and 22.5 (4.5) mg/g, while 3-α-OH steroids are measured at 4.1 (0.4), 4.5 (0.5), 5.3 (0.8), and 4.8 (0.6) mg/g.
